items.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This is a bot-filed documentation gap issue noting that `Azure.Core` README's new "Authenticating Service Clients" section (added in PR #58082) is missing a link to the Migration Guide, which could leave users without troubleshooting guidance when they encounter `CS0433` compile errors or `TypeLoadException` after upgrading to `Azure.Core` 1.53.0.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Core` (1.53.0+)
**Affected API:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` — "Authenticating Service Clients" section
**Scenarios:**
- User upgrades to `Azure.Core` 1.53.0, follows the new README section, but hits `CS0433` compile errors or `TypeLoadException` due to mixed package versions and finds no troubleshooting link in the `Azure.Core` docs
- User arrives via `Azure.Core` documentation and is unaware that `using Azure.Identity;` directives remain valid (types are namespace-forwarded)

**Root ask:** Add a reference to the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md) in the `Azure.Core` README's "Authenticating Service Clients" section, plus a note that `Azure.Identity` namespace directives remain valid.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The gap is documentation-only — no SDK code changes are needed. PR #58082 (commit `0ba3519`) added the "Authenticating Service Clients" section to `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` but did not include a cross-reference to the Migration Guide already present in `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` and `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/CHANGELOG.md`.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` around line 238 (the "Authenticating Service Clients" section) to confirm the Migration Guide link is absent.
2. Compare with `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` to see the existing Migration Guide callout as a reference for the prose to add.
3. Apply the prose addition as specified in the issue's Implementation Guide — no new sample or snippet changes are required.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — the issue is about Azure SDK documentation behavior (README content), not Azure service REST API behavior
- **Service:** `Azure.Core` — the package is explicitly named in the issue title and body
- **Confidence:** High — the package and category are unambiguous; the issue directly references `sdk/core/Azure.Core/`
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Azure.Core` (line 60) — the predicted "Azure.Core" service label matches this entry exactly
- **AzureSdkOwners:** `@m-nash`
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@m-nash` (single AzureSdkOwner); "needs-team-attention" was not added because the issue was filed by a bot (not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top; management-specific entries (lines 912–1230) all require `%Mgmt` and were skipped; no other entry for `%Azure.Core` was encountered before the match at line 60
</details>
